Name: La Cova Fumada
Tips: NO SIGN on door. Cash only. Arrive at 12:00 opening — often sold out by 13:15. Closed weekends and evenings. Invented the Bomba. Order la Bomba + daily fish.

Name: Quimet & Quimet
Tips: Lunchtime only Mon-Sat 12:00-16:00. Standing room only. Arrive at opening. Sardines+yogurt+honey montadito is unmissable. One of Barcelona's best food experiences.

Name: Cal Pep
Address: Pl. de les Olles 8 El Born
Tips: Sit at the bar not the dining room. No menu — chef decides. Go at 13:30 lunch or join 20:30 dinner queue. Bar seats fill fast. One of the most beloved restaurants in Barcelona.

Name: El Xampanyet
Address: C/ de la Montcada 22 El Born
Tips: One of Barcelona's oldest bodegas. House xampanyet cava is the drink. Anchovies are legendary. Arrive at 12:00 opening on weekdays to avoid wait. Packed at weekends.

Name: 7 Portes
Address: Pg. d'Isabel II 14 El Born waterfront
Tips: Barcelona institution since 1836. Book 2-3 weeks ahead for August. Order arrros negre or fideuà. White-jacketed waiters. Special occasion restaurant.

Name: Carrer de Blai (pintxos street)
Tips: Best Basque-style pintxos in Barcelona. Go 18:00-20:00. Try Bar Blai 9 and Tasca el Corral. Kids pick their own from bar displays. Most fun cheap eating in city.

Name: Bar del Pla
Address: C/ de la Montcada 2 El Born
Tips: Vaulted brick interior. Outstanding croquetes de pernil and patatas bravas. Quieter than nearby tourist spots. Great wine list. Go for lunch.

Name: La Pineda
Tips: Open since 1930 — utterly unchanged. Shelves of cured meats cheeses conservas. Wine poured from barrels. Almost no tourists know this place. Tiny — 10 people max.

Name: Bodega Quimet
Tips: Ancient barrels cured meats hanging ceiling. Open since 1920s. Go Sunday 12:00-14:00 for full local vermut ritual. Vermouth on tap + tinned conservas.

Name: Ciudad Condal
Tips: Always buzzing. No reservations — arrive at opening 13:00 or 20:00. Great patatas bravas croquetas gambas al ajillo. Reliable high-volume quality.

Name: Cervecería Catalana
Tips: Lively reliable tapas bar. Great croquetes de bacallà and pa amb tomàquet. Walk-in only — arrive at opening. Good for groups and families.

Name: Mauri Pastissería
Tips: Classic Catalan pastry institution since 1929. Belle-époque interior worth seeing. Order ensaïmada or croissant de mantequilla. Splurge once.

Name: Xurrería Laietana
Address: C/ de la Xucla 4 El Raval
Tips: Generations-old churros bar. Hot chocolate so thick you can stand a spoon in it. Go early morning — closes when sold out. Guaranteed hit with kids.

Name: Bar Calders
Tips: Best morning café in Sant Antoni. Pa amb tomàquet + café amb llet for breakfast. Doubles as excellent vermouth bar at midday. Beloved by locals.

Name: Quimet & Quimet
Tips: See above — listed twice for map visibility (Poble-sec). Lunchtime only Mon-Sat.

Name: Bar Marsella
Address: C/ de Sant Pau 65 El Raval
Tips: Operating since 1820 — oldest bar in Barcelona. Absinthe bar with dusty bottles and peeling mirrors. Hemingway drank here. No music no Wi-Fi. Time capsule.

Name: La Mar Salada
Address: Pg. de Joan de Borbó 58 Barceloneta
Tips: Waterfront paella and fresh seafood. More intimate than 7 Portes. Easier to book. Good terrace.

Name: El Nacional
Tips: Four restaurants four bars under spectacular 19th-century industrial hall. Ideal for families — everyone picks what they want. Walk-in friendly even in August.

Name: Kaiku
Tips: Tiny local-favourite. Possibly best arros negre (squid ink rice) in Barcelona per local food writers. Lunch only. Book ahead — small room.

Name: La Pepita
Address: C/ del Comte Borrell 98 Sant Antoni
Tips: Gourmet bocadillos with creative fillings. Queue moves fast. Great quick lunch between sights. Sobrasada + honey + brie is outstanding.

Name: Bar del Convent
Tips: Hidden café in courtyard of 17th-century convent. Quiet shaded atmospheric. Completely tourist-free. Coffee + pastry in courtyard silence. Tue-Sun.

Name: Forn de Sant Jaume
Tips: Outstanding traditional Catalan pastry shop. Ensaïmada and coca de llardons (pork fat pastry — Catalan specialty). Go at 8am for fresh pastries.

Name: Morro Fi
Address: C/ del Consell de Cent 171 Eixample
Tips: Excellent craft vermouth selection and light tapas. Favourite of local food crowd. More modern than Bodega Quimet but equally good.

Name: Bodega Sepúlveda
Tips: Neighbourhood bodega beloved by locals completely off tourist map. Wine from barrels simple food old regulars. The kind of place you return to daily.

Name: Federal Café
Address: Pg. de Sant Antoni Abat 24 Sant Antoni Barcelona
Tips: Australian-owned specialty coffee and brunch spot in Sant Antoni — pioneer of Barcelona brunch culture. Eggs dishes, sourdough, excellent coffee. Arrive before 10am on weekends to avoid queues.

Name: La Barceloneta Restaurant
Address: Pg. Marítim de la Barceloneta Barceloneta Barcelona
Tips: Traditional chiringuito run by the same family for decades on the Barceloneta waterfront. Utterly authentic — grilled fish, cold beer, plastic chairs, sea air. Best for weekday lunch.

Name: Tickets
Tips: Albert Adrià's famous tapas bar — playful avant-garde takes on Spanish classics. One of the hardest reservations in Barcelona. Book 60 days ahead at midnight on tickets.bar. Worth the effort.

Name: Parking Pizza
Tips: Gràcia neighbourhood pizza restaurant — thin-crust Neapolitan style, outstanding quality, completely non-tourist. Beloved local spot. Good for a relaxed family dinner. Can book or walk in on weeknights.

Name: Koy Shunka
Address: C/ d'en Copons 7 El Gòtic Barcelona
Tips: Barcelona's best Japanese restaurant, tucked behind the cathedral. Japanese chef, top-quality ingredients. Omakase or kaiseki menu. Book weeks ahead. Worth it for a blowout meal.

Name: Honest Greens
Tips: Fresh Mediterranean fast-casual with Spanish ingredients. Great for a quick nourishing lunch that everyone can agree on. Good option for the day after a heavy meal.

Name: DelaCrem
Tips: Cult artisan gelato near the centre — pistachio from Bronte and seasonal fruit sorbets. Family treat after a Gaudi morning.
